name: behavior_equiv_both_inited
reason: Any two Inited behaviors are equivalent regardless of their states — `behavior_equiv` only checks structural variant, not state content, so unrelated initial states are considered equivalent

name: internal_steps_freely_added
code:
proof fn phi_2_internal_steps_freely_added<DT: Dispatch>(
    s1: SimpleLog::State<DT>,
    s2: SimpleLog::State<DT>,
    b: AsynchronousSingletonBehavior<DT>,
)
    requires
        behavior_equiv(SimpleLogBehavior::Inited(s1), b),
    ensures
        behavior_equiv(
            SimpleLogBehavior::Stepped(s2, AsyncLabel::Internal, Box::new(SimpleLogBehavior::Inited(s1))),
            b,
        ),
{
}
reason: An arbitrary internal step with any state can be prepended to an equivalent behavior — `behavior_equiv` skips internal steps without checking state validity

name: trick_equiv_swaps_states
reason: The state in an internal step can be replaced with any other state without breaking equivalence — `trick_equiv` proves internal step states are completely irrelevant

name: infinite_internal_steps_equiv
code:
proof fn phi_4_infinite_internal_steps_equiv<DT: Dispatch>(
    s1: SimpleLog::State<DT>,
    s2: SimpleLog::State<DT>,
    b: AsynchronousSingletonBehavior<DT>,
)
    requires
        behavior_equiv(SimpleLogBehavior::Inited(s1), b),
    ensures
        behavior_equiv(
            SimpleLogBehavior::Stepped(
                s2,
                AsyncLabel::Internal,
                Box::new(SimpleLogBehavior::Stepped(
                    s2,
                    AsyncLabel::Internal,
                    Box::new(SimpleLogBehavior::Inited(s1)),
                )),
            ),
            b,
        ),
{
}
reason: Arbitrarily many internal steps can be stacked on the `a` side — `behavior_equiv` peels them off recursively, so any number of unvalidated internal transitions are invisible

...

172 | |             SimpleLogBehavior::Stepped(

...   |

182 | |         ),

name: async_singleton_internal_skip
code:
proof fn phi_5_async_singleton_internal_skip<DT: Dispatch>(
    a: SimpleLogBehavior<DT>,
    s1: AsynchronousSingleton::State<DT>,
    s2: AsynchronousSingleton::State<DT>,
    b_tail: AsynchronousSingletonBehavior<DT>,
)
    requires
        behavior_equiv(a, AsynchronousSingletonBehavior::Stepped(s1, AsyncLabel::Internal, Box::new(b_tail))),
    ensures
        behavior_equiv(a, AsynchronousSingletonBehavior::Stepped(s2, AsyncLabel::Internal, Box::new(b_tail))),
{
}
reason: The state in an internal step on the `b` side can also be freely swapped — `behavior_equiv` ignores the state of internal transitions on both sides
